Undisputed WWE Championship

Details
WWE Title History

On this page, you find the full Title History for the Undisputed WWE Championship, with all Championship Info, Statistics, the Belt Design changes through the years, and the full list of Title Reigns, along with dates, events, and reign durations.

The Undisputed WWE Championship is a World Title contested in World Wrestling Entertainment on the SmackDown brand, by men wrestlers.

The championship was first established on April 25, 1963, and the inaugural champion was "Nature Boy" Buddy Rogers.

Since that time, the biggest names in the business have held the title, including Bruno Sammartino, Andre the Giant, Bret Hart, The Rock and "Stone Cold" Steve Austin. The WWE Championship is widely recognized as the most historic championship in WWE.

The World Heavyweight Championship ('02-'13), WCW World Heavyweight Championship and WWE Universal Championship were previously unified into this championship.

The current champion is CM Punk, who won the title on the episode of Raw on July 6, 2026, and has been reigning for 10 days.

Undisputed WWE Championship: Title History

  • Undisputed WWE Championship: Title Reigns
  • Undisputed WWE Championship: Names & Belt Designs
  • Undisputed WWE Championship: Statistics

Undisputed WWE Championship: Title Reigns

    This was a Three Stages of Hell match.

    days

    John Cena 14

    April 20, 2025 Paradise, NV | WWE WrestleMania 41
    105
    days
    147

    Cody Rhodes 1

    April 7, 2024 Philadelphia, PA | WWE WrestleMania XL
    The title was being collectively defended with the Universal Championship as the Undisputed WWE Championship.
    378
    days
    146

    Roman Reigns 4

    April 3, 2022 Arlington, TX | WWE WrestleMania 38
    The then-WWE Universal Champions Roman Reigns defeated Brock Lesnar in a Winner Takes All match. The titles remained independently active, but were collectively defended on any brand as the Undisputed WWE Universal Championship. The titles (as Undisputed WWE Universal Championship) became exclusive to SmackDown after the 2023 WWE Draft.

    AJ Styles 1

    September 11, 2016 Richmond, VA | WWE Backlash 2016

    140
    days
    127

    Dean Ambrose 1

    June 19, 2016 Las Vegas, NV | WWE Money in the Bank 2016
    Cashed in the Money in the Bank contract. The title's name reverted to WWE Championship. The brand extension returned and the title became exclusive to the SmackDown brand following the 2016 WWE Draft, and then renamed to WWE World Championship.

    November 5, 2015
    The title was vacated after Seth Rollins suffered a torn ACL, MCL and a damaged meniscus.

    Daniel Bryan 3

    64
    days
    116

    Randy Orton 8

    October 27, 2013 Miami, FL | WWE Hell in a Cell 2013
    Defeated Daniel Bryan in a Hell in a Cell match to win the vacant title. At TLC, Orton defeated John Cena to unify the World Heavyweight Championship with the WWE Championship; the World Heavyweight Championship was then retired and the WWE Championship became known as the WWE World Heavyweight Championship.
    161
    days

    Vacant

    September 16, 2013 Cleveland, OH | Raw
    The title was vacated after a controversial finish when Daniel Bryan defeated Randy Orton to win the title.

    Vacant

    7
    days
    104

    CM Punk 1

    July 17, 2011 Rosemont, IL | WWE Money in the Bank 2011
    Punk (kayfabe) "left" WWE the day after with the championship, but he returned on the July 25, 2011 episode of Raw and his reign was deemed to continue through this period. He defeated John Cena in a match to determine the undisputed champion at SummerSlam.

    Vacant

    Immediately after winning the title, André the Giant handed the championship belt to Ted DiBiase, but this was ruled as vacating the title.
    51
    days
    11

    André the Giant 1

    February 5, 1988 Indianapolis, IN | WWF The Main Event
    0
    days
    10

    Hulk Hogan 1

    January 23, 1984 New York, NY | WWF on MSG Network
    1474
    days
    9

    The Iron Sheik 1

    December 26, 1983 New York, NY | WWF on MSG Network
    The title was renamed WWF World Heavyweight Championship when WWF ended its affiliation with NWA.
    28
    days
    8

    Bob Backlund 1

    February 20, 1978 New York, NY | WWF on MSG Network
    The title was renamed WWF Heavyweight Championship when WWWF became WWF in March 1979. Backlund lost the title to Antonio Inoki in Japan in 1979 but regained the belt within a week, and the WWF never acknowledged the title change in the USA.
    2135
    days
    7

    "Superstar" Billy Graham 1

    April 30, 1977 Baltimore, MD | House Show
    296
    days
    6

    Bruno Sammartino 2

    December 10, 1973 New York, NY | House Show
    1237
    days
    5

    Stan Stasiak 1

    December 1, 1973 Philadelphia, PA | House Show
    9
    days
    4

    Pedro Morales 1

    February 8, 1971 New York, NY | House Show
    The title was renamed WWWF Heavyweight Championship when WWWF rejoined NWA in 1971.
    1027
    days
    3

    Ivan Koloff 1

    January 18, 1971 New York, NY | House Show
    21
    days
    2

    Bruno Sammartino 1

    May 17, 1963 New York, NY | House Show
    2803
    days
    1

    "Nature Boy" Buddy Rogers 1

    April 11, 1963 Washington, D.C. | Heavyweight Wrestling
    Rogers won the NWA World Heavyweight Championship on June 30, 1961; in 1963 Northeast promoters refused to recognize, thus breaking away from the NWA to form the WWWF and recognizing Rogers as the first WWWF World Heavyweight Champion.
    36
    days
